name: DICK WRAY
title: Abstract Expressionist Painter

Bibliography

  • Johnson, Patricia C. "Abstract Mind at Work," Houston Chronicle, February 13, 2000.
  • Kalil, Susie. "Dick Wray: The Painting is the Thing," Art in America, June 1997.

  • Barnes, Steve. "Dick Wray: Chaos and Order," Artweek, September 1984.

Legacy

Dick Wray (1933-2011) was recognized as one of Houston's most significant abstract expressionist painters. His energetic, gestural works were characterized by bold brushstrokes, vibrant colors, and complex layering techniques that captured the energy and chaos of the modern world.

Throughout his career spanning more than five decades, Wray influenced generations of Texas artists and helped establish Houston as an important center for contemporary art.
